title: Military Aviation MRO Expansion
entry_mechanism: Alsalam expansion as primary vehicle + PSAP-attracted international MRO specialists
executive_summary: Saudi Arabia operates one of the largest military aviation fleets in the world — approximately 850 aircraft — with the majority of heavy maintenance and engine overhaul performed outside the Kingdom. Node MA-2 targets full-spectrum in-Kingdom MRO capability across heavy maintenance, engine shop overhaul, and component repair. The strategic case is existential: operational sovereignty requires the ability to maintain the fleet without foreign permission. The economic case is equally compelling: $1.8–2.4 billion per year in MRO spending currently leaving the Kingdom represents a massive import substitution and high-skill employment opportunity.
